@@ -11,6 +11,7 @@ When the user asks for commits or you finish a logical unit of work in this repo
 2. **One concern per commit** — Keep commits small and reviewable: e.g. schema/migrations separate from UI, API routes separate from unrelated styling.
 3. **Message quality** — Use clear imperative subjects (what/why in 1–2 sentences in the body when helpful). Do not commit secrets (`.env`, `.env.local`, `.insforge/`, credentials).
 4. **Verify** — Run relevant tests or typecheck when the change warrants it; run `git status` after committing to confirm a clean tree for that slice.
-5. **Push** — Only push when the user explicitly requests it.
+5. **Explain** — Run the `explain-diff` skill for that commit (`node scripts/understanding-diff.mjs --commit HEAD`). See `understanding-layer` rule.
+6. **Push** — Only push when the user explicitly requests it.
 
 Prefer **multiple small commits** over **one large commit**, unless the user asks for a single squashed commit.
diff --git a/.cursor/rules/understanding-layer.mdc b/.cursor/rules/understanding-layer.mdc
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..f40d738
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.cursor/rules/understanding-layer.mdc
@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+---
+description: After each incremental commit, generate the understanding layer (explainer + quiz)
+alwaysApply: true
+---
+
+# Understanding layer (human-in-the-loop)
+
+Agents that change code must help the human **understand and verify** work as it lands — not only at PR time.
+
+## After every focused commit
+
+1. **Commit** the code slice (see `incremental-commits` rule).
+2. **Run** `explain-diff` skill for that commit:
+   - `node scripts/understanding-diff.mjs --commit HEAD`
+   - Write `docs/understanding/commits/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<short-sha>-<slug>/explainer.html` and `explainer.md`
+3. **Quiz** is mandatory in every explainer (5 questions by default).
+4. If hands-on learning would help, **propose** a micro-world (`explain-micro-world` skill) — build only after human picks an option.
+
+Do not defer explainers until the feature is "done". Understanding debt compounds like technical debt.
+
+## When the branch / PR is ready
+
+1. **Run** `explain-diff` for the full branch vs base (`main`):
+   - `node scripts/understanding-diff.mjs --branch <branch> --base main`
+   - Write `docs/understanding/prs/<branch-slug>/explainer.html` and `explainer.md`
+2. Link to per-commit explainers in the PR explainer index.
+3. Mention the understanding docs in the PR description under **Review guide**.
+
+## Principles (non-negotiable)
+
+- Plain language; define jargon on first use.
+- Background → intuition → code (literate diff, not raw diff dump).
+- Interactive quiz at the end — reading alone is not enough.
+
+## Paths
+
+| Artifact | Location |
+|----------|----------|
+| Commit explainer | `docs/understanding/commits/<date>-<sha>-<slug>/` |
+| PR explainer | `docs/understanding/prs/<branch-slug>/` |
+| Templates | `docs/understanding/_templates/` |
+| Reusable micro-world patterns | `docs/understanding/_patterns/` |
+
+Commit explainers **are** versioned with the repo so reviewers can follow the story commit-by-commit.
diff --git a/docs/agents/understanding-layer.md b/docs/agents/understanding-layer.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..ea22092
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/agents/understanding-layer.md
@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+# Understanding layer
+
+How humans stay in the loop while agents ship code — explainers, quizzes, and micro-worlds.
+
+## Problem
+
+Long agent runs produce large diffs. Raw `git diff` is hard to review, easy to skim without understanding, and impossible to "quiz yourself" on. Context compounds across commits.
+
+## Solution
+
+An **understanding layer** generated **incrementally**:
+
+| When | What | Skill |
+|------|------|-------|
+| Each focused commit | Literate diff + quiz (+ optional micro-world proposal) | `explain-diff` |
+| Hands-on exploration needed | Interactive playground | `explain-micro-world` |
+| PR ready for review | Roll-up explainer linking commit docs | `explain-diff` |
+
+## Layout
+
+```
+docs/understanding/
+├── README.md
+├── _templates/          # HTML + Markdown scaffolds
+├── _patterns/           # Reusable micro-world patterns (human-approved)
+├── commits/
+│   └── 2026-07-02-a1b2c3d-add-feed-filter/
+│       ├── explainer.html
+│       ├── explainer.md
+│       ├── diff-context.json   # machine-readable diff summary (optional)
+│       └── micro-world/        # if built
+└── prs/
+    └── cursor-feature-d250/
+        ├── explainer.html
+        ├── explainer.md
+        └── index.md              # links to commit explainers
+```
+
+## For humans
+
+1. After an agent commits, open the matching folder under `docs/understanding/commits/`.
+2. Read **Background** and **Intuition** first.
+3. Skim the **Code** walkthrough.
+4. Take the **Quiz** — if you miss questions, re-read or ask for a micro-world.
+5. At PR time, read the roll-up under `docs/understanding/prs/`.
+
+Open `explainer.html` in a browser for the interactive quiz.
+
+## For agents
+
+See `.cursor/rules/understanding-layer.mdc` and skills:
+
+- `.cursor/skills/explain-diff/SKILL.md`
+- `.cursor/skills/explain-micro-world/SKILL.md`
+
+Helper:
+
+```bash
+node scripts/understanding-diff.mjs --commit HEAD
+node scripts/understanding-diff.mjs --branch my-branch --base main
+```
+
+## Design influences
+
+- [Geoffrey Litt — explain-diff](https://gist.github.com/geoffreylitt/a29df1b5f9865506e8952488eac3d524) — literate diffs + embedded quizzes
+- Andy Matuschak & Michael Nielsen — spaced repetition inside essays
+- Seymour Papert — *Mathland* / micro-worlds for learning by inhabiting
+
+## PR description snippet
+
+When opening a PR, include:
+
+```markdown
+## Review guide
+
+- **PR explainer:** docs/understanding/prs/<branch>/explainer.html
+- **Per-commit:** docs/understanding/prs/<branch>/index.md
+```
diff --git a/docs/understanding/README.md b/docs/understanding/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e8b27f9
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/understanding/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,29 @@
+# Understanding layer
+
+Teaching documents for agent-written code — one folder per commit and per PR.
+
+## Quick start
+
+| I want to… | Open |
+|------------|------|
+| Understand the latest commit | `commits/` — newest dated folder |
+| Review a whole branch | `prs/<branch-slug>/explainer.html` |
+| Copy the HTML scaffold | `_templates/explainer.html` |
+| See micro-world ideas | `commits/.../micro-world-proposal.md` |
+
+## Conventions
+
+**Folder names**
+
+- Commits: `YYYY-MM-DD-<7-char-sha>-<kebab-slug>`
+- PRs: branch name with slashes replaced by `-` (e.g. `cursor-feature-d250`)
+
+**Files**
+
+- `explainer.html` — full interactive doc (preferred for reading)
+- `explainer.md` — same content, git-friendly
+- `diff-context.json` — output of `scripts/understanding-diff.mjs` (for agents)
+
+## Agents
+
+Read `docs/agents/understanding-layer.md` and use the `explain-diff` skill after each commit.

+# Micro-world patterns
+
+Reusable forms the human has approved for this repo. Copy and adapt when proposing new micro-worlds.
+
+*(None yet — add a pattern here when a micro-world form works well and should be repeated.)*
+
+## How to add a pattern
+
+1. Build a micro-world for a commit; human confirms the form is worth reusing.
+2. Add a subsection below with: **name**, **best for**, **template path**, **example commit**.
+
+### Example entry (template)
+
+#### Step-through toy API
+
+- **Best for:** New API routes, request/response shaping
+- **Template:** `docs/understanding/_patterns/toy-api-playground/`
+- **Example:** `docs/understanding/commits/…/micro-world/`
